headline: Energy Leads as War Premium Grips Midday Trade; Consumer and Homebuilders Bear the Brunt
body_html:
Thursday's midday session is unfolding under a clear geopolitical shadow, with Energy the lone sector posting meaningful gains — up 0.85% — as escalating conflict in the Middle East pushes oil prices higher and rewrites the calculus for commodity-linked names. TotalEnergies' report of its strongest quarterly profit in nearly three years, alongside Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers and a twelfth consecutive night of U.S. strikes on Iran, has traders pricing in a sustained supply disruption premium. Breadth is notably negative across the 501-name universe, with 290 issues lower against 207 advancing.

The session's standout mover is Medpace, surging 16.69% in healthcare-CRO, followed by Thermo Fisher's 10.02% advance in life sciences — both suggesting earnings-driven rotation into defensive growth. Allegion and Union Pacific are also among the session's brightest spots, each up roughly 10% and 6.5% respectively, lending Industrials a modest positive read of plus 0.13%.

On the downside, Tesla is off nearly 12% and Rollins drops 12.55% to lead decliners, while the Homebuilders sector — weighed by NVR's 5.9% slide — is the hardest-hit group at minus 2.36%. Consumer names are under additional pressure following Albertsons' warning that softening grocery spending will weigh on its full-year results. Into the close, watch energy names and any fresh Middle East headlines for direction.

briefs.headline: Medpace Rockets to Session Highs on Earnings Surge
briefs.body: Contract research firm Medpace is the undisputed standout of Thursday's session, vaulting 16.69% by midday. The move signals strong investor appetite for healthcare-services names that can demonstrate durable revenue visibility, drawing rotational interest away from broader software and semiconductor weakness.

briefs.headline: Albertsons Warns of Softer Consumer Amid Grocery Spending Slowdown
briefs.body: Albertsons rattled consumer-staples investors after cautioning that weakening grocery expenditure will weigh on both sales and full-year earnings. The warning aligns with the Consumer sector's midday decline of 1.46%, underscoring how price-sensitive shoppers are pulling back even on everyday necessities.

briefs.headline: Iran War Keeps Oil Routes in Focus; Energy Sector Outperforms
briefs.body: Houthi forces claimed strikes on Saudi tankers near Bab el-Mandeb, while two Chinese supertankers carrying Saudi crude approached the contested strait. The threat of a second chokepoint disruption — on top of Red Sea tensions — is amplifying the geopolitical risk premium that has Energy leading every sector midday.

briefs.headline: American Airlines Trims 2026 Profit Outlook as Jet Fuel Costs Climb
briefs.body: American Airlines cut its full-year earnings guidance, citing a sharp rise in fuel costs that is compressing operating margins. The revision adds to evidence that the Iran conflict's oil-price spillover is reaching transportation balance sheets, and may prompt further guidance adjustments across the airline industry.

briefs.headline: ECB Deliberations Stoke September Rate-Hike Speculation
briefs.body: Traders are increasingly pricing in a European Central Bank rate increase at the September meeting after officials flagged concern over an energy-driven inflation spike. The shift in rate expectations is adding a cross-Atlantic dimension to Thursday's risk tone and pressuring rate-sensitive growth equities.

briefs.headline: Rollins and Tesla Lead the Decliner Board; Homebuilders Extend Losses
briefs.body: Pest-control firm Rollins fell 12.55% — the steepest drop in the universe — while Tesla shed nearly 12%, making the auto-tech name the second-largest decliner. Homebuilder NVR's 5.9% slide contributed to the sector's 2.36% midday loss, reflecting broader caution around discretionary and rate-sensitive spending.
